verdict_reasoning: HSPA5/GRP78/BiP is canonically an ER-lumenal chaperone retained by its KDEL motif. However, under ER stress conditions — including hypoxia, nutrient deprivation, tumorigenesis, and viral infection — it escapes ER retention and translocates to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane, where it functions as a co-receptor and signaling hub. Surface GRP78 is well-documented on multiple cancer cell types (prostate, breast, ovarian) by non-permeabilized flow cytometry and surface biotinylation assays, and is targeted by antibody therapeutics. This is a classic oncogenic/stress-state-induced ecto-form of a nominally intracellular protein.
